description: Two attempts at the only fair meta-tools test cell: puzzle_004 scratchpad with meta=on (agent self-extension nudge), via run_plan (windows W8, W9).
motivation: Give the meta-tools axis a fair test (the earlier "inert" claim was confounded - the meta server was never configured) on the one puzzle where scratchpad's clean bail should make it affordable.
formal_results: Both aborted on the rolling 5h usage limit mid-trial: W8 $6.17 in 18.5 min, W9 $18.61 in 24.5 min - burn rate implies a completed trial would cost $25-40+. No completed meta-on trial; ~$39 wasted across attempts.
results_description: The meta-nudge suppresses the v2 clean-bail, so the only puzzle where meta is affordable is exactly the one whose meta-nudge makes it cost-unbounded.
conclusions: No fair, cost-bounded meta test exists on subscription compute. The meta axis is dropped from the main matrix (footnote/appendix); a positive result would be a separate paper on non-subscription compute.
report: Full detail on the two meta-on probe attempts (W8 $6.17, W9 $18.61, both rate-limit aborts) and the firm decision to drop the meta axis as cost-infeasible on subscription compute is in the stream-level report (source: notes/2026-05-18_aaai_plan_and_meta_confound.md, sections "Meta-axis confound" and "Meta axis: dropped").
